name: Hide UI Elements on Freshservice Catalog Items
description: Fully automated: Hide specific UI elements (like Place Request button) on Freshservice service catalog item pages. Uses playwright_cli to navigate Portal Designer, inject SPA-safe CSS via CodeMirror API, save, publish, and verify. Requires item_ids[] and css_selectors as input parameters.

item_ids.description: Freshservice catalog item display_ids to target (e.g. [604, 607])

element_description.description: Human-readable name of what's being hidden (e.g. 'Place Request button')

steps.description: Open Portal Designer in headed Chrome session
steps.notes: Start a headed browser session so Freshservice session cookies from the default Chrome profile are available. Must run before any other steps.
Direct URL to Portal Designer. If redirected to login, Freshservice session cookie must be active in Playwright browser.

steps.description: Take snapshot to find the Head section link in the sidebar
steps.notes: Look for a clickable element labeled 'Head' in the Portal Designer sidebar. Store its ref for step 3.

steps.description: Click the Head section link in the sidebar
steps.note: Run snapshot first; replace ref with actual element ref from snapshot output
steps.notes: Use the ref from the snapshot in step 2.

steps.description: Read current Head content via CodeMirror API
steps.notes: CodeMirror textarea is not directly clickable — must use the CodeMirror JS instance. This gets the existing content so we can append to it (not overwrite existing meta tags etc).

steps.description: Build the SPA-safe script and inject it via CodeMirror API. Appends to existing Head content. Uses setInterval polling to add/remove style on URL match — prevents SPA navigation bleed.
steps.notes: CRITICAL: Use setInterval add/remove pattern, NOT one-shot inject. One-shot causes SPA bleed bug — style persists in DOM across client-side navigations and hides element on ALL catalog items. Discovered 2026-03-10 v1 bug reported by Jeannette Vivas.

steps.description: Click Save button in Portal Designer
steps.notes: Take snapshot first to find the Save button ref, then click it.

steps.note: Run snapshot first; replace ref with actual element ref from snapshot output

steps.description: Click Publish button to push changes live
steps.notes: Take snapshot to find Publish button ref after save completes.

steps.note: Run snapshot first; replace ref with actual element ref from snapshot output
steps.notes: Both Save AND Publish required. Save alone doesn't push to production.

steps.description: Verify: Navigate to target catalog item and confirm element is hidden
steps.notes: Navigate to the first target item to verify the element is hidden. Replace {first_item_id} with the first value from item_ids at runtime.

steps.description: Take snapshot of target catalog item page to confirm element is not in DOM / not visible
steps.notes: Check that the target elements (e.g. 'Service Item Footer' region, place-request-btn) are NOT visible in the snapshot. If they appear, the script failed.

steps.description: SPA bleed test: Navigate to a DIFFERENT catalog item via in-app link (not direct URL) and confirm element IS visible there
steps.notes: Navigate to any catalog item NOT in the target list. The Place Request button MUST be visible here. If hidden, the SPA bleed bug is back — revert immediately.

steps.description: Take snapshot of non-target catalog item to confirm element IS visible (SPA bleed check)
steps.notes: The 'Service Item Footer' / place-request-btn MUST be visible here. If not visible = SPA bleed bug, need to revert.

tool_params.message_template: Completed: {element_description} hidden on catalog items {item_ids}. Portal saved and published. Verified hidden on target items and visible on non-target items (SPA bleed check passed).

steps.notes: If any verification step failed, report the failure and revert instructions instead.
Close the headed browser session after all steps complete.

rollback.description: To revert: Go to Portal Designer Head section, remove the <script> block for the target items, Save and Publish.
steps: Read current content via CodeMirror API
Remove the script block for the target catalog items
Write updated content back via cm.setValue()

known_issues.description: One-shot style injection persists across SPA client-side navigations, hiding element on ALL catalog items
known_issues.fix: Use setInterval polling with add/remove pattern — adds style when on target URL, removes when navigating away

known_issues.description: Playwright cannot click the textarea — CodeMirror presentation layer intercepts clicks
known_issues.fix: Access via document.querySelector('.CodeMirror').CodeMirror JS API, use cm.setValue() and cm.refresh()

known_issues.description: The Place Request element is <input type='button'> not <button> — querySelectorAll('button') won't match
known_issues.fix: Use CSS class selectors (.place-request-btn) which work regardless of element type
